incidentLog.location: Port of Rotterdam, Berth ECT Delta
incidentLog.title: Container Lashing Failure - Stack Collapse
incidentLog.description: During heavy weather transit from Hamburg to Rotterdam, 3 containers in bay 42 experienced lashing rod failure. One 40ft container shifted approximately 15cm. No cargo damage reported. Discovered during pre-arrival inspection.
incidentLog.rootCause: Inadequate tension on lashing rods; possible fatigue in turnbuckle assembly
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Re-secured all affected containers; replaced damaged lashing equipment; crew retrained on lashing inspection procedures
incidentLog.reportedTo: Port State Control Rotterdam, Classification Society

incidentLog.title: Phosphine Gas Detection - Cargo Hold 3
incidentLog.description: During routine gas monitoring, elevated phosphine levels detected in cargo hold 3 (12 ppm). Hold was fumigated at departure from Santos for grain cargo. Ventilation system activated immediately. No personnel exposure.
incidentLog.rootCause: Fumigation seals degraded; insufficient hold sealing prior to departure
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Emergency ventilation for 48 hours; fumigation contractor reviewed; new sealing procedures implemented
incidentLog.reportedTo: Flag State, Next Port Authority (Le Havre), IMO Fumigation Database

incidentLog.location: Port of Le Havre, Terminal GMP
incidentLog.title: Undeclared Dangerous Goods Discovery
incidentLog.description: During random container inspection, lithium batteries (Class 9) found in container declared as 'General Merchandise - Textiles'. Container originated from shipper in Shanghai.
incidentLog.rootCause: Shipper declaration error; possible intentional misdeclaration
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Container quarantined; proper DG documentation obtained; shipper reported to authorities; enhanced screening implemented

incidentLog.title: Pilot Ladder Non-Compliance
incidentLog.description: During Suez Canal pilot embarkation, pilot observed that pilot ladder step spacing did not meet SOLAS requirements. Pilot refused transfer until replacement ladder deployed.
incidentLog.rootCause: Worn pilot ladder not identified during last inspection; ladder exceeded service life
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Ladder replaced immediately; all pilot transfer equipment inspected; new inspection checklist implemented per MSC circular

incidentLog.title: Twistlock Failure During Discharge
incidentLog.description: Automated twistlock failed to release during container discharge operation. Container suspended approximately 2m above vessel deck for 45 minutes before manual release.
incidentLog.rootCause: Mechanical failure in automated twistlock mechanism; manufacturing defect suspected
incidentLog.correctiveAction: All twistlocks from same batch inspected; supplier notified; manual backup procedures reinforced

incidentLog.title: Oxygen Deficiency - Ballast Tank Entry
incidentLog.description: During ballast tank inspection, oxygen level dropped to 18.5% during entry. Two crew members evacuated immediately. No injuries. Entry permit was valid but ventilation was insufficient.
incidentLog.rootCause: Inadequate ventilation during entry; gas monitoring frequency insufficient
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Enhanced ventilation procedures; continuous gas monitoring implemented; crew retrained per revised A.1050(27)

incidentLog.title: IMSBC Schedule Mismatch - Direct Reduced Iron
incidentLog.description: DRI cargo shipped with incorrect IMSBC schedule reference. Cargo declared under old schedule; new amendments (08-25) not applied. Moisture content certification incomplete.
incidentLog.rootCause: Shipper unaware of updated IMSBC requirements; port facility not updated on new schedules
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Cargo re-sampled and re-certified; updated documentation obtained; shipper educated on IMSBC amendments

incidentLog.title: Lashing Software Discrepancy
incidentLog.description: Cargo securing manual calculations did not match lashing software output for heavy machinery cargo. Discrepancy of 12% in calculated forces.
incidentLog.rootCause: Lashing software using outdated parameters; CSM not updated with latest software version
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Software updated; calculations verified by chief officer; manual verification protocol established

incidentLog.title: IMDG Column 17 Information Missing
incidentLog.description: Multiple containers with chemicals lacked required Column 17 information on shipping documents (flashpoint, toxicity level). Discovered during document check.
incidentLog.rootCause: Shipper documentation incomplete; freight forwarder not aware of updated IMDG requirements
incidentLog.correctiveAction: All documentation corrected before loading; freight forwarder training required; enhanced document verification

incidentLog.title: Container Stack Weight Exceeded
incidentLog.description: During loading, container stack weight exceeded bay limit by 8 tonnes. Discovered by terminal software. Loading sequence had to be re-planned causing 4-hour delay.
incidentLog.rootCause: Stowage plan not properly validated; weight verification step skipped
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Stowage plan re-validated; weight verification mandatory step added; terminal software integration improved

incidentLog.title: Fumigation Warning Sign Missing
incidentLog.description: Container with fumigated cargo lacked proper warning signs at all access points. Discovered during pre-loading inspection.
incidentLog.rootCause: Terminal staff failed to apply warning signs after fumigation
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Signs applied; container isolated until proper ventilation confirmed; terminal staff retrained

incidentLog.title: Free-Fall Lifeboat Release Mechanism Defect
incidentLog.description: During monthly inspection, free-fall lifeboat release mechanism showed signs of corrosion and incomplete engagement. Simulated launch test failed.
incidentLog.rootCause: Inadequate maintenance; saltwater ingress into release mechanism
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Release mechanism replaced; all lifeboat systems inspected; maintenance schedule enhanced per MSC circular requirements

incidentLog.title: Fish Meal Cargo - Self-Heating Risk Undeclared
incidentLog.description: Fish meal cargo shipped without antioxidant treatment declaration. Cargo began self-heating during voyage from Peru. Temperature monitoring detected rise to 55°C.
incidentLog.rootCause: Shipper failed to declare antioxidant treatment status; IMSBC schedule requirements not followed
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Emergency ventilation; cargo temperature monitored continuously; port authorities notified; cargo rejected at destination
incidentLog.reportedTo: Greek Coast Guard, Flag State, IMO

incidentLog.title: Pilot Hoist Found in Use
incidentLog.description: Vessel found using mechanical pilot hoist during pilot embarkation. Pilot refused transfer. Hoist removed and proper ladder arrangements deployed.
incidentLog.rootCause: Chief officer unaware of SOLAS V/23 amendments prohibiting mechanical pilot hoists
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Hoist permanently removed; new pilot transfer arrangements installed; crew trained on SOLAS amendments

incidentLog.title: Suspicious Vessel Approach - Piracy Alert
incidentLog.description: Two small craft approached vessel at high speed in known piracy area. BMP5 procedures activated. Vessel increased speed, deployed fire hoses. Craft broke off after 20 minutes.
incidentLog.rootCause: Transit through high-risk area despite piracy advisories
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Route adjusted; MSCHOA notified; armed security team embarked at next port

incidentLog.title: Lashing Rod Thread Damage
incidentLog.description: Multiple lashing rods found with damaged threads during pre-loading inspection. Equipment removed from service.
incidentLog.rootCause: Normal wear and tear; inspection interval exceeded
incidentLog.correctiveAction: All lashing equipment inspected; damaged equipment replaced; inspection interval reduced to 6 months

incidentLog.title: Lithium Battery Fire Risk - Improper Packaging
incidentLog.description: Container with lithium batteries found with damaged packaging and some batteries showing signs of thermal runaway. Container isolated on deck.
incidentLog.rootCause: Inadequate packaging for lithium battery transport; IMDG Code packing instructions not followed
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Container isolated and monitored; fire department standby; cargo offloaded for re-packaging; shipper penalized

incidentLog.title: New IMSBC Schedule Not Applied - Zinc Slag
incidentLog.description: Zinc slag cargo shipped under old generic schedule instead of new specific schedule from IMSBC 08-25 amendments.
incidentLog.rootCause: Port facility and shipper not updated on new IMSBC schedules effective Jan 2026
incidentLog.correctiveAction: Documentation updated; cargo re-classified under new schedule; shipper educated

incidentLog.title: Gas Monitor Calibration Expired
incidentLog.description: During routine audit, portable gas monitors found with expired calibration certificates. Equipment removed from service.
incidentLog.rootCause: Calibration schedule not properly tracked; responsible officer changed without proper handover
incidentLog.correctiveAction: All gas monitors recalibrated; calibration tracking system implemented; spare equipment procured

items.workPerformed: Engine overhaul; davit inspection; release gear testing
items.findings: Engine reconditioned; release gear certified per MSC.1/Circ.1693

items.workPerformed: Annual lashing equipment replacement program

items.workPerformed: Drone system procurement and crew training

items.findings: Wire ropes replaced; safety nets renewed; hydraulic system serviced

items.workPerformed: Complete system overhaul; new sensors installed
items.findings: All fixed and portable detectors replaced with new calibrated units

items.workPerformed: Installation per MSC.1/Circ.1691
items.findings: New towing pennants installed; chafing gear renewed; procedures updated

items.workPerformed: Annual certification per MSC.1/Circ.1696
items.findings: All cranes and lifting equipment load tested; documentation updated

items.workPerformed: Cargo hold modifications for new bulk cargo schedules

items.workPerformed: Update to version 5.0 with harmonized performance standards

keyTrends: Lashing equipment failures concentrated in heavy weather transits
Fumigation incidents related to phosphine gas and sealing issues
Dangerous goods declaration errors increasing with new IMDG requirements
IMSBC Code amendment transition causing documentation issues
Pilot transfer arrangements required major upgrade for SOLAS V/23 compliance
Life-saving appliance maintenance costs increased due to new MSC circular requirements
recommendations: Implement quarterly lashing equipment inspection cycle
Enhance fumigation contractor vetting and seal verification procedures
Mandatory DG documentation training for all loading ports
IMSBC Code 08-25 amendment awareness program for shippers
Complete pilot transfer arrangement upgrades fleet-wide
Implement gas monitor calibration tracking system
